data.body:
[G4 child-consistency re-audit â€” parent safe-luge-nog/b4d4-3369-6dd2-41e6, THIS child (908c link-consumer) vs whole roster (908c link-consumer + ba2c parent-CLEAR consumer)]

Re-checked the 7 cross-child interaction modes (high-confidence, benign-reading filter applied); all CLEAN. Verified against code:

1. Implicit shared state â€” `managed_refs` + `should_propagate_removal` are EXPLICITLY parent-owned shared state (parent What/Scope). Confirmed via search: `managed_refs` has zero matches in the tree â†’ parent builds it; both children only READ the gate/set. No hidden/undeclared shared mutation. CLEAN.

2. Conflicting assumptions â€” gate semantics agree across parent + both children: peer ref present + locally absent â‡’ REMOVE/CLEAR iff âˆˆ managed_refs, else ADOPT inbound; local-authoritative, NO inbound-remove dual. 908c ("NO inbound-remove dual â€¦ consistent with parent") matches ba2c (CLEAR only when âˆˆ managed_refs, else ADOPT) and parent's `should_propagate_removal`. CLEAN.

3. Dependency gap â€” 908c logically depends on the parent primitive/gate; both children carry parent_id b4d4 (the primitive builder), supplying ordering. Parent Scope explicitly tracks the two CONSUMERS as children. CLEAN.

4. Scope overlap â€” 908c = LINK path (`_diff_links`, new acli `delete_relationship`, batch_dispatch link REMOVE, inbound link-ADD suppression); ba2c = PARENT-CLEAR path (`_map_local_to_jira_fields`/`emit_detach_clear`). Disjoint code paths. Root-cause cites verified accurate: outbound `_diff_links` ADD-only ("No REMOVE mutations are emitted", outbound_differ.py:1020-1021); inbound `_diff_links_inbound` ADD-only ("No REMOVE mutations", inbound_differ.py:463-464). Shared gate is common CONSUMPTION, not duplicated logic. CLEAN.

5. Ordering violation â€” 908c and ba2c are independent consumers (links vs parent); no inter-child ordering dependency; both gated behind the parent primitive. CLEAN.

6. Consumer impact â€” both children extend same-pass inbound suppression, but on DISJOINT keys: 908c uses the link-add echo set `link_add_keys` (inbound_differ.py:599-601,619-623), ba2c uses the scalar `fields`/`parent`â†’`parent_id` path (_OUTBOUND_TO_INBOUND_FIELD, inbound_differ.py:578-581,633-644). No cross-interference. CLEAN.

7. Residual references â€” 908c references parent alias "safe-luge-nog"; confirmed b4d4's alias IS safe-luge-nog. No stale/dangling reference. CLEAN.

Re-affirming the prior NON-G4 note (within-child / G2-class, NOT a cross-child contradiction): the Jira "Blocks" type collapses both `blocks` and `depends_on` logical kinds direction-agnostically (inbound_differ.py:497-502; outbound `_existing_jira_links` adds both inward/outward keys, outbound_differ.py:995-998), while parent-defined `managed_refs` keys on direction-distinguished `(kind, target)`. 908c's link-REMOVE gate lookup against a direction-collapsed Jira "Blocks" link cannot deterministically reconstruct whether to probe `(blocks, target)` or `(depends_on, target)` â€” an underspecification INTERNAL to 908c's gate consumption. It does NOT contradict sibling ba2c (parent ref is single-direction `(parent, target)`, unambiguous), so it is a within-child gap, not a cross-child interaction defect. Confirmed G2-class, out of scope for G4.

Result: no high-confidence cross-child interaction defect. Parent + this child are consistent. G4 CLEAN (empty findings).
